message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To study the features of creatine-kinase (CK) in COVID-19 patients with different ages, clinical types and outcomes and quantify the relationship between CK value and clinical type.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>All laboratory confirmed COVID-19 patients hospitalized in Xiangyang No.1 People’s Hospital were included. Patients’ general information, clinical type, all CK values and outcome were collected.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The peak median value of CK in cases aged ≥ 71 years old (appeared at T2) was higher than that in cases aged ≤ 70 years old. There was statistical difference between the two groups (
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    =0.001). Similarly, the peak in critical cases (appeared at T2) was higher than moderate and severe types, and significant difference were existed among moderate, severe, and critical types (
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    =0.000). Moreover, the peak value in death group (appeared at T2) was higher than those in survival group. Significant difference was also found between them (
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    =0.000). According to the optimal scale regression model, the CK value (
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    =0.000) and age (
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    =0.000) were associated with the clinical type.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Difference of the CK in different ages, clinical types, and outcomes were significant. The results of the optimal scale regression model are helpful to judge the clinical type of COVID-19 patients.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Features of creatine-kinase in COVID-19 patients with different ages, clinical types and outcomes: A cohort study
